comments.body:
I'm optimizing Parameter Golf on final_int8_zlib_roundtrip_exact val_bpb (lower better), int8+zlib artifact ≤16MB. On Runpod I use run_experiment.py so each GPU run appends autoresearch/results.tsv.

1x H100 baseline-1gpu: low-rank / factorization sweeps over embedding, MLP (FC and projection), and attention-query paths; int8 export clip-percentile brackets, LeakyReLU-squared MLP, validation experiments (sliding stride vs full val with caps). Strongest harness row so far: val_bpb 1.3297, int8+zlib ≈14.35MB (results.tsv 2026-03-25, 070-equivalent SEED12345). I no longer have that run's full train.log locally—later jobs overwrote current_submission/train.log. Reproduced internal baseline val_bpb ≈1.351 (Gate B, 2026-03-21 1×H100). Three seeds on one anchored recipe span ~0.007 bpb.

The draft PR pairs train_gpt.py + train.log for the same run as my latest logged row: val_bpb 1.35151831, int8+zlib 12,622,882 bytes (600s wall, seed 12345, late-window int8 fake-quant + partial-graph torch.compile)—see submission.json in the PR folder.

I do not claim README ~1.12 parity—different val semantics, codecs, eval cost; I compare on the contest metric when eval knobs match.

Starter $25 Runpod credits exhausted; ~$47 self-funded. I need 8× H100 timed train+eval and multi-seed checks. First funded block: one hypothesis on final-8gpu with kill rules (byte cap, eval wall, no gain vs fixed RUN_ID). Then one-axis compression work (fake-quant vs export clip) with RUN_IDs.
